scenario_results.reason: Handled a complete fintech servicing case with sponsor-bank, ledger, processor, approval, and customer-communication evidence and produced an audit-ready completion record.
Blocked progression when ledger, processor, settlement, or ACH exception artifacts were incomplete and returned a retrieval-owned exception summary.
Refused completion when final-state confirmation, action logs, or approval and communication evidence were missing.
Placed the work item in a blocked state when upstream approval or processor confirmation was still pending and preserved explicit unblock criteria.
Moved a divergent ledger and processor case into rework or escalation instead of silently forcing the next state.
Normalized sponsor-bank, FBO, BIN, wallet ledger, ACH, and settlement terminology into fintech-specific servicing output.
Routed fraud, Reg E adjudication, KYC, sanctions, and legal requests to the correct adjacent specialists without claiming that authority.
Returned low-confidence escalation when authority ownership or source-of-truth precedence was missing for a customer-impacting step.
Rejected overgeneralized fintech handling and required actual product, rail, program, system, and authority context.
Escalated conflicting public and tenant policy guidance rather than synthesizing an unsupported operational answer.
Refused unsupported fund-release, fee-waiver, notice-suppression, and back-date override requests and preserved the current execution state.
Tenant-specific adaptation preserved separate sponsor-bank and processor role maps and blocked unsafe blended completion behavior.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass against a 0.92 minimum threshold for validated fintech and embedded-finance operations handling.
Functionality coverage is complete across all shared and execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities required by the resolved cross-industry profile.
